metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4623
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1e-h. confocal microscopy of primary hippocampal neurons that are triply immunostained for snapin, EHD1 and synaptophysin show partial overlap of all 3 proteins, indicating that EHD1 and snapin colocalize with presynaptic terminal marker synaptophysin.

Further assays show that EHD1 and SNAP25 interact with the same interaction-domain of snapin (fig 3,4), providing additional indirect evidence for presynaptic localization of snapin. Note that unlike EHD1, there is no immunogold data for snapin in this paper.. Experimental description: "Immunoelectron microscopy
Four male Wistar rats of approximately 200 g each were perfused with 4% paraformaldehyde and 0.1% glutaraldehyde in 0.1 M phosphate buffer. The brains and spinal cords were removed and sectioned coronally at 100 μm thickness using a Vibratome. The floating sections were washed for 3 h in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to remove traces of fixative and immersed for 1 h in a solution of 5% normal goat serum in PBS to block non-specific binding of antibodies. They were then incubated overnight with affinity purified antibody to EHD1 (Xu et al., 2004), diluted 1:100 in PBS."

"The EHD1 antibody was prepared and characterized as previously described (Lowe et al., 1996; Xu et al., 2001). In brief, rabbits were injected with 300 μg of purified recombinant GST-EHD1 emulsified in complete Freund's adjuvant, followed by booster injections containing similar amounts of the antigen emulsified in incomplete Freund's adjuvant at 2-week intervals. The animals were bled 10 days after the third and subsequent booster injections and the serum was affinity purified by incubation with cyanogen bromide-activated-sepharose beads coupled with GST-EHD, and antibodies eluted from the beads (Xu et al., 2004)."
